goal: Make effectiveness part of done by adding a standing orphan gate to the WLP. Add a recognized optional field orphan_gate (off | warn | enforce, default off). In --verify, after the forbidden and outside-allowed checks, when enforce, fail the task if a module THIS task added or changed under builder/src is not live-reachable via USED value imports and carries no '// @orphan-by-design: <reason>' tag; warn reports without failing. The gate evaluates only the current task's changed set, never the legacy backlog, makes no network calls, and treats type-only, test-only and unused/side-effect-only imports as non-connection. Its guarantee is honestly bounded: usage is checked heuristically, not proven.
scope_out: Do not make the gate enforce by default; default is off. Do not evaluate or fail on modules outside the current task's changed set. Do not add any network or provider call; static analysis only. Do not change builder/src application code. Do not trigger the gate via reuse_target; the only trigger is the orphan_gate field. Do not claim the gate proves runtime invocation; it checks heuristic usage and reports residual blind spots.
claims: validateContractFields can accept a new optional field orphan_gate in {off, warn, enforce} without breaking existing contracts, which omit it.
runVerify can host an orphan-gate check after checkForbidden and checkOutsideAllowed without changing behavior when orphan_gate is off or absent.
tools/orphan-scan.cjs can be required as a library to compute live-reachability via used value imports for the changed set.
type-only, test-only and unused/side-effect-only imports are reported separately and do NOT satisfy the gate; an unused value import cannot make the gate green.
the gate's usage detection is a heuristic; it may miss dynamic dispatch or re-export chains, which is documented rather than hidden.
assumptions: tools/orphan-scan.cjs exists from WIRE-CENSUS-001 and exposes connectivity fields including serverReachable, usedValueImporters, unusedValueImporters, typeImporters, testImporters.
baseline_ref is set to the WIRE-CENSUS-001 commit f067c14.
The current task's changed set comes from getChangedFiles(baseline_ref).
dod: validateContractFields recognizes optional field orphan_gate in {off, warn, enforce}; absence means off; an invalid value fails validation with a clear message.
runVerify runs the orphan-gate check after checkForbidden and checkOutsideAllowed, active only for warn or enforce; off or absent leaves behavior unchanged.
The gate evaluates ONLY changed/added non-test files under builder/src in the current diff; modules outside the diff are never evaluated.

Connectivity uses orphan-scan: a changed module passes only if serverReachable is true from the single explicit root builder/src/server.ts; orchestratorReachable, type-only, test-only and unused/side-effect-only imports are listed but do not count.

Under enforce, a non-connected changed module without a '// @orphan-by-design: <reason>' top-of-file tag exits non-zero naming the file; under warn it prints a warning and exits zero; tagged modules pass and are printed as accepted exceptions.

tools/orphan-gate.test.cjs covers: non-connected new module fails under enforce; used-value-connected new module passes; type-only-only new module fails; UNUSED value-import-only new module fails; @orphan-by-design module passes; warn mode does not fail; a legacy module outside the diff is not evaluated.
docs/WLP-ORPHAN-GATE-v0.1.md documents the field, the tag format, the used-value connectivity rule, the changed-set-only scope and the heuristic's residual blind spots.
node --test tools/orphan-gate.test.cjs, task-lock verify and diff-check pass.
stop_conditions: Making the gate enforce by default for all contracts becomes necessary.
The gate would need to evaluate the legacy backlog or fail on files outside the task diff.
The gate would need a network call, a provider call, full type-checking, or any builder/src application change.
prior_task_findings: WIRE-CENSUS-001 delivered tools/orphan-scan.cjs with separate connectivity fields and the used-value usage check. The legacy lattice is handled by census triage, not by this gate, which only stops new orphans.
